docs.title: Sorsa
docs.text: Testing and documentation for the Sorsa API integration.

docs.title: Guides
docs.text: • Ingestion Pipeline

docs.title: Mindshare Data Ingestion Pipeline
docs.text:
Document Scope

This document covers the complete data ingestion pipeline for the Mindshare backend — from the daily scheduled trigger, through the Sorsa/TweetScout API calls that fetch the last 3 days of tweets, all the way to the final AI analysis and database state. Target audience: engineers onboarding to this codebase or debugging production data gaps.

docs.title: 1. Architecture Overview
docs.text:
The system is a tweet data processing and analysis pipeline. Every day it:

  1. Fetches raw tweets for every tracked crypto project via the Sorsa/TweetScout API (3-day rolling window)
  2. Stores raw and processed posts in PostgreSQL
  3. Refreshes analytical materialized views
  4. Recalculates time-decay mindshare scores
  5. Runs LLM-powered sentiment analysis and content scoring

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text: Layer Technology Role Task Queue Huey + Redis Scheduling & task distribution Primary DB PostgreSQL Permanent structured data Document Store MongoDB (Beanie) Raw tweet cache, score cache Data Lake AWS S3 (Parquet) Archive for replay/backfill Tweet Source Sorsa / TweetScout API v3 Tweet search and user scores AI Analysis LiteLLM → OpenRouter → Grok-4.1-fast Sentiment & content scoring Cache Redis Response caching & rate limiting

docs.title: High-Level Architecture
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Trigger["⏰ Trigger Layer"]
        CRON["Cron: daily 00:06 UTC"]
        HUEY["Huey Redis Queue"]
        SCHED["scheduler.py\nschedule_daily_update_mindshare_data()"]
    end

    subgraph Fetch["📥 Data Fetch Layer"]
        WORKER["mindshare_data_worker.py\nupdate_mindshare_data()"]
        SERVICE["mindshare/service.py\nupdate_tracked_projects_data(until_days=3)"]
        SORSA["Sorsa / TweetScout API v3\n(search-tweets, comments, user-tweets)"]
        SORSASCORE["Sorsa API v3\n(/score-id/{x_id})"]
    end

    subgraph Store["💾 Storage Layer"]
        RAW["raw_data.raw_post\n(JSONB)"]
        PG["mindshare.mindshare_post\n(PostgreSQL)"]
        PGUSER["mindshare.mindshare_user\n(PostgreSQL)"]
        MONGO["MongoDB: Tweets, TweetScoutScore"]
    end

    subgraph Compute["⚙️ Compute Layer"]
        VIEWS["Materialized View Refresh\nanalytics.refresh_engagement_views_all()"]
        DECAY["Decay Score Recalc\nmindshare_score.calculate_all_decay_scores()"]
        SNAPS["Snapshot Views\nsnapshots.create_all_snapshots_views()"]
    end

    subgraph AI["🤖 AI Layer"]
        SENTIMENT["Sentiment Analysis\nGrok-4.1-fast, batch=10"]
        CONTENT["Content Scoring\nGrok-4.1-fast, batch=5, multimodal"]
    end

    subgraph Cache["🔄 Cache Layer"]
        REDIS_CACHE["Redis Cache Invalidation\nget_v2_mindshare_leaderboard\nget_mindshare_leaderboard_snapshot_from_mv"]
    end

    CRON --> SCHED --> HUEY --> WORKER
    WORKER --> SERVICE
    SERVICE -->|"POST /search-tweets"| SORSA
    SERVICE -->|"POST /comments"| SORSA
    SERVICE -->|"GET /score-id/{x_id}"| SORSASCORE
    SORSA --> RAW
    RAW --> PG
    SORSASCORE --> PGUSER
    PG --> VIEWS
    VIEWS --> DECAY --> SNAPS
    PG --> SENTIMENT --> PG
    PG --> CONTENT --> PG
    SNAPS --> REDIS_CACHE
    SERVICE --> MONGO

docs.title: Cron Schedule
docs.text:
The entire pipeline is kicked off by a single Huey periodic task defined in app/worker/scheduler.py:

@huey.periodic_task(
    crontab(minute="06", hour="00")  # Daily at 00:06 UTC
)
def schedule_daily_update_mindshare_data():
    tasks.daily_update_mindshare_data_task.call_local()

Huey runs as a separate process (huey_consumer.py) with 4 threads, backed by Redis. Tasks survive worker restarts because they persist in the Redis queue.

docs.title: Task Chain
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Redis
    participant Scheduler as scheduler.py
    participant Tasks as tasks.py
    participant Worker as mindshare_data_worker.py
    participant Service as mindshare/service.py

    Redis->>Scheduler: Fire crontab(00:06 UTC)
    Scheduler->>Tasks: daily_update_mindshare_data_task.call_local()
    note over Tasks: @huey.task(retries=1, retry_delay=60)
    Tasks->>Worker: update_mindshare_data()
    Worker->>Service: update_tracked_projects_data(until_days=3)
    note over Service: Production: 3 days lookback<br/>Development: 0.01 days (≈14 min)

docs.title: Environment Branching
docs.text:
The lookback window is environment-sensitive (app/worker/mindshare_data_worker.py:386-391):

if CONFIG.ENABLE_TRACKED_PROJECTS_UPDATE and CONFIG.ENVIRONMENT == Environments.DEVELOPMENT:
    await update_tracked_projects_data(0.01)   # ~14 minutes for fast local testing
elif CONFIG.ENABLE_TRACKED_PROJECTS_UPDATE:
    await update_tracked_projects_data(3)       # 3 calendar days in production

docs.title: Feature Flags
docs.text:
All three major phases can be independently disabled:

Flag Default Effect when False ENABLE_TRACKED_PROJECTS_UPDATE True Skips all API calls and DB ingestion ENABLE_SENTIMENT_ANALYSIS True Skips LLM sentiment scoring ENABLE_RELEVANCY_SCORING True Skips LLM content scoring

docs.title: API Roots
docs.text:
Two base URLs are involved. Both authenticate with the same TWEETSCOUT_API_KEY, passed as the ApiKey HTTP header.

Config Key Base URL Purpose TWEETSCOUT_BASE_URL Configured externally Tweet search, replies, user-tweets SORSA_BASE_URL https://api.sorsa.io/v3 User influence scores

v3 Migration

The codebase migrated from TweetScout v2 to Sorsa v3 in April 2026 (commit e46092d). Many field names changed — see the Data Transformation section for the full mapping.

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: All tweet endpoints are implemented in app/modules/tweetscout/third_party_tools/tweetscout_api.py.

docs.title: POST /search-tweets — Keyword Search
docs.text:
async def get_tweets_by_keyword(
    tweet_query: str, cursor: str | None
) -> Tuple[List[Dict], str | None]:
Parameter Type Value query string Project keyword (e.g. "Acurast") order string "latest" — newest first next_cursor string Pagination cursor (omitted on first page)

Returns (tweets[], next_cursor). Timeout: 30 s.

docs.title: POST /comments — Reply Thread Fetch
docs.text:
async def get_tweetscouts_replies(
    tweet_query: str, tweet_link: str, cursor: str | None
) -> Tuple[List[Dict], str | None]:
Parameter Type Value tweet_link string Full URL of the parent tweet next_cursor string Pagination cursor

Returns (tweets[], next_cursor). Timeout: 30 s.

docs.title: POST /user-tweets — Per-User Timeline
docs.text:
Used for syncing tracked project team members (KOLs). Timeout: 30 s.

Parameter Type Value user_id string X user ID next_cursor string Pagination cursor

docs.title: GET /score-id/{x_id} — User Influence Score
docs.text:
async def request_tweetscout_profile(x_id: str) -> dict:
    url = f"{CONFIG.TWEETSCOUT_BASE_URL}/score-id/{x_id}"

Returns user profile with Sorsa influence score. Timeout: 90 s. Gracefully handles "User not found" and protected accounts by returning score: 0.

docs.title: 3-Day Fetch Window
docs.text:
The cutoff date is computed in app/modules/mindshare/service.py:302:

now_utc = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
cutoff_date = now_utc - timedelta(days=until_days)   # until_days=3 in prod

await store_project_tweets_replies_and_users(word, cutoff_date, now_utc)

The API is paginated newest-first. The pagination loop reads cursor pages until the oldest tweet in a batch is older than cutoff_date. This guarantees all tweets within the window are captured even if the API returns them out of order.

flowchart LR
    A["now_utc"] -->|"- 3 days"| B["cutoff_date"]
    B --> C{{"oldest tweet in page\n< cutoff_date?"}}
    C -->|No| D["fetch next page"]
    D --> C
    C -->|Yes| E["stop pagination"]

docs.title: 4. Full Ingestion Flow
docs.text:
This sequence diagram shows one project's complete ingestion run from scheduler trigger to final database commit.

sequenceDiagram
    participant Sched as scheduler.py
    participant Worker as update_mindshare_data()
    participant Service as update_tracked_projects_data()
    participant TSService as tweetscout_service.py
    participant TSAPI as tweetscout_api.py (Sorsa)
    participant PG as PostgreSQL
    participant Mongo as MongoDB

    Sched->>Worker: daily trigger (00:06 UTC)
    Worker->>Service: update_tracked_projects_data(until_days=3)

    note over Service: asyncio.Semaphore(2)<br/>max 2 projects concurrently

    loop for each active project (keyword)
        Service->>TSService: store_project_tweets_replies_and_users(keyword, cutoff_date, now)

        note over TSService: fetch_and_create_tweets_until()<br/>max 10 000 cursor iterations

        loop cursor pagination
            TSService->>TSAPI: POST /search-tweets {query, order:"latest", next_cursor}
            TSAPI-->>TSService: {tweets[], next_cursor}
            TSService->>PG: insert_raw_posts() → raw_data.raw_post (JSONB)
            TSService->>TSService: format_tweets() + filter_invalid_posts()
            TSService->>PG: upsert_mindshare_post() → mindshare.mindshare_post
            TSService->>TSService: check: oldest tweet < cutoff_date?
        end

        loop for each tweet (replies)
            TSService->>TSAPI: POST /comments {tweet_link, next_cursor}
            TSAPI-->>TSService: {tweets[], next_cursor}
            TSService->>PG: upsert replies → mindshare.mindshare_post
        end

        note over Service: fetch missing user scores
        Service->>TSAPI: GET /score-id/{x_id} (concurrency=5)
        TSAPI-->>Service: {score, username, followers_count, ...}
        Service->>PG: upsert_mindshare_user()

        Service->>Service: update_avatar_and_username_until(days=4)
        Service->>Mongo: create_mindshare_snapshots() (6 time frames)
    end

    note over Service: sync tracked KOL users (0.1 day lookback)
    Service->>TSService: sync_multiple_users_background(user_id_list, limit=100)

docs.title: Concurrency Model
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A["active_projects (all keywords)"]
    A --> B["asyncio.gather(*tasks)\nall projects start concurrently"]
    B --> C["asyncio.Semaphore(2)\nmax 2 running at once"]
    C --> D1["Project A: fetch_and_create_tweets_until"]
    C --> D2["Project B: fetch_and_create_tweets_until"]
    C -.->|"waits for slot"| D3["Project C (queued)"]

    D1 --> E1["Score fetch\nSemaphore(5) parallel"]
    D2 --> E2["Score fetch\nSemaphore(5) parallel"]

docs.title: Sorsa v3 Field Mapping
docs.text:
The v3 API (Sorsa) changed many field names from the legacy TweetScout v2 format. The formatter in app/modules/tweetscout/crud.py handles this:

v2 Field v3 Field (Sorsa) PostgreSQL Column id_str id post_id user.id_str user.id user_x_id user.screen_name user.username user_x_username user.name user.display_name user_x_display_name user.avatar user.profile_image_url user_x_avatar_url favorite_count likes_count favorite_count in_reply_to_status_id_str in_reply_to_tweet_id replied_post_id retweeted_status.id_str retweeted_status.id retweeted_post_id quoted_status.id_str quoted_status.id quoted_post_id

docs.title: Raw API Response Shape (Sorsa v3)
docs.text:
{
  "id": "1234567890",
  "created_at": "2026-05-07T10:30:00Z",
  "full_text": "Tweet content here",
  "reply_count": 5,
  "retweet_count": 10,
  "quote_count": 2,
  "likes_count": 50,
  "view_count": 1200,
  "in_reply_to_tweet_id": "9876543210",
  "retweeted_status": { "id": "..." },
  "quoted_status": { "id": "..." },
  "entities": { "media": [...], "urls": [...] },
  "user": {
    "id": "user_id_string",
    "username": "handle",
    "display_name": "Full Name",
    "profile_image_url": "https://pbs.twimg.com/...",
    "followers_count": 1000
  }
}

docs.title: Transformation Pipeline
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A["Sorsa API
raw JSON response"] --> B["insert_raw_posts()
→ raw_data.raw_post
(full JSONB blob)"]
    B --> C["format_tweets()
Adds tweet_created_at,
tweet_query fields"]
    C --> D["filter_invalid_posts()
Validates against
project's keyword rules"]
    D --> E["upsert_mindshare_post()
→ mindshare.mindshare_post
ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE"]
    E --> F["ensure_partition_exists()
Creates per-project
PG partition if needed"]

docs.title: mindshare.mindshare_post Schema (Key Columns)
docs.text: Column Source Notes post_id tweet.id PK (with post_created_at) project_keyword tweet_query Partition key post_created_at tweet.created_at PK component full_text tweet.full_text Raw tweet text user_x_id tweet.user.id FK → mindshare_user view_count tweet.view_count favorite_count tweet.likes_count v3 rename retweet_count tweet.retweet_count reply_count tweet.reply_count entities tweet.entities JSONB, used for image extraction is_reply computed in_reply_to_tweet_id IS NOT NULL is_retweet computed retweeted_status IS NOT NULL sentiment_score LLM (post-ingestion) Filled by AI pipeline content_score LLM (post-ingestion) Filled by AI pipeline

docs.title: HTTP-Level Retries
docs.text:
Implemented in app/modules/tweetscout/third_party_tools/utils.py:

  • Max retries: 3 attempts
  • Retry on: HTTP 5xx server errors only
  • No retry on: HTTP 4xx client errors (raised immediately as HTTPErrorWithData)
  • Retry delay: 2 seconds between attempts

docs.title: Pagination-Level Retries
docs.text:
Implemented in app/modules/tweetscout/tweetscout_service.py:107-221:

flowchart TD
    A["Call /search-tweets with cursor"] --> B{{"Response OK?"}}
    B -->|Yes, got tweets| C{{"next_cursor empty?"}}
    C -->|No| D{{"oldest tweet < cutoff_date?"}}
    D -->|No| A
    D -->|Yes| DONE["Stop — 3-day window reached"]
    C -->|Yes| E{{"cursor_retry_count < 3?"}}
    E -->|Yes| F["sleep(3^n)\n3s → 9s → 27s\ncursor_retry_count++"]
    F --> A
    E -->|No| G["Stop — cursor exhausted"]
    B -->|HTTPErrorWithData| H["save_post_fetch_state(status=failed)"]
    H --> STOP["Break loop"]
    B -->|Unexpected error| I{{"unexpected_retry < 3?"}}
    I -->|Yes| J["sleep(3^n), retry"]
    J --> A
    I -->|No| H

docs.title: Task-Level Retries
docs.text: daily_update_mindshare_data_task is configured with retries=1, retry_delay=60 in app/worker/tasks.py — the entire pipeline retries once after 60 seconds if it throws an unhandled exception.

docs.title: 7. Post-Ingestion Processing Pipeline
docs.text:
After raw data is stored, update_mindshare_data() continues with four sequential compute steps before AI analysis:

flowchart TD
    INGEST["✅ Tweet ingestion complete\n(mindshare.mindshare_post populated)"]

    INGEST --> VIEWS["refresh_all_engagement_views()\nTransaction 1: analytics.refresh_engagement_views_all()\n  → mv_engagement_{keyword}\nTimeout: 30 min\n\nTransaction 2: mindshare_score.refresh_engagement_features_views_all()\n  → mv_engagement_features_{keyword}\nTimeout: 60 min"]

    VIEWS --> DECAY["calculate_decay_scores_all()\nPostgreSQL: mindshare_score.calculate_all_decay_scores()\nApplies time-decay weighting to engagement scores"]

    DECAY --> SNAPS["create_snapshot_for_projects_v2()\nPostgreSQL: snapshots.create_all_snapshots_views()\nCreates/refreshes 6 materialized views per project:\n  {keyword}_24hr, {keyword}_7d, {keyword}_30d\n  {keyword}_3m, {keyword}_6m, {keyword}_1yr"]

    SNAPS --> SENT{{"ENABLE_SENTIMENT_ANALYSIS?"}}
    SENT -->|True| SENTIMENT["process_tweets_sentiment_analysis()\nbatch_size=10, page_size=100"]
    SENT -->|False| CONTENT_GATE

    SENTIMENT --> CONTENT_GATE{{"ENABLE_RELEVANCY_SCORING?"}}
    CONTENT_GATE -->|True| CONTENT["process_tweets_content_scoring()\nbatch_size=5, page_size=100"]
    CONTENT_GATE -->|False| CACHE

    CONTENT --> CACHE["invalidate_all_fn_cache()\n• get_v2_mindshare_leaderboard\n• get_mindshare_leaderboard_snapshot_from_mv"]
    SENTIMENT -.->|False branch| CACHE

docs.title: 8.1 Sentiment Analysis
docs.text:
File: app/worker/utils/mindshare_ai_score_worker.py

Targets all posts where sentiment_score IS NULL AND is_reply=False AND is_retweet=False.

flowchart TD
    A["fetch_posts_without_sentiment(page_size=100)
SELECT post_id, project_keyword,
post_created_at, full_text
FROM mindshare.mindshare_post
WHERE sentiment_score IS NULL
  AND is_reply=FALSE
  AND is_retweet=FALSE"] --> B["Build TweetData list
(tweet_id, full_text only)"]
    B --> C["llm_service.analyze_sentiment_batch()
Model: grok-4.1-fast via OpenRouter
Temperature: 0.05
batch_size=10 tweets per LLM call"]
    C --> D["Returns SentimentResult[]
(tweet_id, sentiment_label, sentiment_score)"]
    D --> E["bulk_update_sentiment_scores()
SQL VALUES bulk UPDATE
→ mindshare.mindshare_post
columns: sentiment_score, sentiment_label"]
    E --> F{{"updated_count == 0?"}}
    F -->|No, more pages| A
    F -->|Yes, done| G["Return stats
{total_processed, total_updated}"]

Output labels: positive, negative, neutral with a confidence score 0.0–1.0.

docs.title: 8.2 Content Scoring
docs.text:
File: app/worker/utils/mindshare_ai_score_worker.py

Targets all posts where content_score IS NULL AND is_retweet=False AND is_reply=False. Projects in the exclusion list (IronAllies_, _technotainment, D3lMundos, Pact_Swap, Acurast, YOM_Official) are skipped.

flowchart TD
    A["fetch_posts_without_content_score(page_size=100)
Groups by project_keyword"] --> B["extract_image_urls(entities)
Max 2 photos per post"]
    B --> C["_validate_image_urls()
aiohttp HEAD requests, timeout=5s
Strips broken URLs before LLM call"]
    C --> D["llm_service.score_content_batch()
Model: grok-4.1-fast (multimodal)
Temperature: 0.1, max_tokens=16384
batch_size=5, max_concurrency=7
Injects project name, description, handle"]
    D --> E["Returns ContentSignalResult[]
9 signals (0-100 scale)"]
    E --> F["compute_content_score()
→ content_score float 0-100"]
    F --> G["bulk_upsert_content_scores()
Transaction:
  UPDATE mindshare.mindshare_post
    SET content_score = ...
  UPSERT mindshare.post_content_signal
    (all 9 signals + reason)
    ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE"]

docs.title: 8.3 Content Score Algorithm
docs.text:
Implemented in app/worker/utils/content_score.py.

Inputs (all 0–100 unless noted):

Signal Description relevance How directly the tweet discusses the project context_depth Informational or analytical substance meme_communication_value How well a meme communicates about the project visual_information_density Concrete project info in images human_signal Genuine expression vs. templated promotion project_mentions Count of crypto projects mentioned (raw count, not 0-100) mention_farming_risk Likelihood tweet exists purely to farm tags ai_generated_probability Likelihood text is AI-generated sentiment -1 to 1 (separate from scoring pipeline)

Algorithm (6 steps):

flowchart TD
    S1["Step 1: Derive communication_value C
C = clamp(max(context_depth, meme_comm_value)
       + 0.25 × visual_info_density, 0, 100)"]
    S2["Step 2: Hard Gate
IF relevance < 20 → content_score = 0"]
    S3["Step 3: Quality score Q
Q = 0.70 × C + 0.30 × human_signal"]
    S4["Step 4: Relevance gate G
G = Q × (relevance/100)^1.3"]
    S5["Step 5: Conditional mentions penalty
Penalizes heavy multi-tagging
ONLY when quality is low AND farming risk is high
Floor: 0.65"]
    S6a["Step 6a: Farming risk penalty
Active above 30 risk score
Floor multiplier: 0.20"]
    S6b["Step 6b: AI generation penalty
1 - (ai_probability / 300)
Floor: 0.70"]
    S7["Final: content_score = clamp(result, 0, 100)"]

    S1 --> S2 --> S3 --> S4 --> S5 --> S6a --> S6b --> S7

post_content_signal table stores all 9 raw signals plus the LLM's reason text, enabling score debugging and recomputation without re-calling the LLM.

docs.title: 9. S3 Backfill Path (Alternative Ingestion)
docs.text:
In addition to the live API path, an alternative ingestion route reads historical data from S3 Parquet files.

File: app/worker/athena_ingestion_worker.py Task: s3_tweets_ingestion_task(user_id=None, fetch_all=False) in app/worker/tasks.py

flowchart LR
    S3["S3 Parquet files
Prefixes:
  tweetscout/
  tweetscout_replies/
Partitioned by:
  user_id/year/month/day/"] --> LIST["List files
(filter by user_id,
date range)"]
    LIST --> READ["Read batches
(500 rows via pyarrow)"]
    READ --> FMT["athena_to_postgres_formatter()
→ NucleusPost
categorize_project_keywords()"]
    FMT --> PG1["UPSERT nucleus_post"]
    FMT --> PG2["UPSERT mindshare_post"]
    FMT --> PG3["UPSERT mindshare_user"]

This path is triggered manually or via s3_tweets_ingestion_task — it is not part of the daily scheduled run.

docs.title: 10. Configuration Reference
docs.text: All environment variables are defined in app/config.py as a Pydantic EnvConfig model.

docs.title: API Keys & External Services
docs.text: Variable Required Description TWEETSCOUT_BASE_URL Yes Base URL for Sorsa/TweetScout tweet search endpoints TWEETSCOUT_API_KEY Yes API key for all TweetScout/Sorsa endpoints SORSA_BASE_URL Yes https://api.sorsa.io/v3 — Sorsa score endpoint base OPENROUTER_API_KEY Yes LiteLLM key for Grok-4.1-fast via OpenRouter COOKIE3_API_KEY No On-chain data provider SNAG_API_KEY No Loyalty platform sync

docs.title: Databases
docs.text: Variable Required Description DATABASE_URL Yes MongoDB connection string POSTGRES_DATABASE_URL Yes PostgreSQL async connection string REDIS_URL Yes Redis URL for Huey task queue and response cache DATABASE_NAME Yes MongoDB database name

docs.title: AWS / S3
docs.text: Variable Required Description AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID For S3 AWS credentials AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY For S3 AWS credentials AWS_REGION For S3 AWS region S3_BUCKET For S3 General data lake bucket TWEETS_S3_BUCKET For S3 Tweet archive bucket

docs.title: Feature Flags
docs.text: Variable Default Description ENABLE_TRACKED_PROJECTS_UPDATE True Enable live API ingestion ENABLE_SENTIMENT_ANALYSIS True Enable LLM sentiment scoring ENABLE_RELEVANCY_SCORING True Enable LLM content scoring ENVIRONMENT production Controls 3-day vs 0.01-day window RATE_LIMIT_EXEMPT_KEYS [] API keys exempt from rate limiting

docs.title: 11. Key Files Reference
docs.text: File Purpose Key Functions / Classes app/worker/scheduler.py Cron schedule definition schedule_daily_update_mindshare_data() app/worker/tasks.py Huey task wrappers with retry config daily_update_mindshare_data_task(), s3_tweets_ingestion_task() app/worker/huey_config.py Redis-backed Huey instance huey (RedisHuey) app/worker/mindshare_data_worker.py Main pipeline orchestration update_mindshare_data() app/modules/mindshare/service.py Project-level ingestion logic update_tracked_projects_data(), create_snapshot_for_projects_v2() app/modules/tweetscout/tweetscout_service.py Pagination loop, score concurrency fetch_and_create_tweets_until(), create_tweetscout_scores_in_parallel() app/modules/tweetscout/third_party_tools/tweetscout_api.py Sorsa HTTP API client get_tweets_by_keyword(), get_tweetscouts_replies(), request_tweetscout_profile() app/modules/tweetscout/tweetscout.py Fetch → raw store → format → upsert fetch_and_create_tweetscout_tweets(), retry_fetch_tweets() app/modules/tweetscout/crud.py PostgreSQL CRUD for posts/users upsert_mindshare_post(), insert_raw_posts() app/modules/mindshare_analytics/service.py Materialized view refresh, decay scores refresh_all_engagement_views(), calculate_decay_scores_all() app/worker/utils/mindshare_ai_score_worker.py LLM sentiment & content scoring workers process_tweets_sentiment_analysis(), process_tweets_content_scoring() app/worker/utils/llm_service.py LiteLLM integration LLMService, analyze_sentiment_batch(), score_content_batch() app/worker/utils/content_score.py Deterministic score formula compute_content_score(), ContentScoreConfig app/worker/athena_ingestion_worker.py S3 Parquet backfill path ingest_s3_tweets_data() app/config.py All environment configuration EnvConfig, CONFIG

docs.title: 12. End-to-End Data Lifecycle Summary
docs.text:
timeline
    title Daily Pipeline Timeline (approximate, production)
    00:06 : Cron fires
           : Huey dequeues task
    00:06 - ~02:00 : Live API ingestion per project
                   : Sorsa /search-tweets (cursor pages until 3-day cutoff)
                   : Sorsa /comments for reply threads
                   : User score fetch via /score-id
                   : raw_data.raw_post written
                   : mindshare.mindshare_post upserted
                   : MongoDB snapshots created
    ~02:00 - ~04:00 : Engagement view refresh
                    : mv_engagement_{keyword} refreshed
                    : mv_engagement_features_{keyword} refreshed
    ~04:00 : Decay score recalculation
           : All project leaderboards updated
           : Snapshot materialized views created
    ~04:00 onwards : LLM Sentiment Analysis
                   : Grok-4.1-fast, batch 10, paginated
    Ongoing : LLM Content Scoring
            : Grok-4.1-fast, batch 5, multimodal
            : 9 signals → content_score
    End : Redis cache invalidated
        : Leaderboard APIs serve fresh data

Debugging a Data Gap

If a project's leaderboard looks stale:

  1. Check raw_data.raw_post for recent rows with that project_keyword — confirms whether the API call succeeded.
  2. Query mindshare.mindshare_post WHERE project_keyword = '...' ORDER BY post_created_at DESC LIMIT 10 — confirms transform and upsert.
  3. Check PostFetchState in MongoDB for fetch_status = 'failed' — records cursor + timestamp of the last failed fetch.
  4. Verify ENABLE_TRACKED_PROJECTS_UPDATE=True in the running environment.
  5. Check Huey worker logs for HTTPErrorWithData or unexpected error messages around 00:06 UTC.
